tips.name: Pomodoro Technique
tips.description: Work 25 min, break 5 min. Repeat 4 times, then long break.

steps.title: Choose a task
steps.description: Pick one specific task you want to accomplish.
steps.fullDescription: Select one specific task you want to accomplish. Write it down if needed. Make sure it's a single task that can be completed in one Pomodoro session.
steps.tip: Start with the most important task of your day.

steps.title: Set timer 25 min
steps.description: Focus only on that task.
steps.fullDescription: Start the timer for 25 minutes. During this time, focus only on your chosen task. No phone, no social media, no distractions.
steps.tip: Put your phone away. Close unnecessary tabs.

steps.title: Take 5 min break
steps.description: Step away from your desk.
steps.fullDescription: Step away from your desk. Stretch your legs, drink water, close your eyes. Do anything that helps you relax, but avoid screens.
steps.tip: Walk around, stretch, drink water. No screens!

steps.title: Repeat 4 times
steps.description: After 4 cycles, take a long break.
steps.fullDescription: After 4 Pomodoros (about 2 hours), take a longer break of 15-30 minutes. Use this time to rest, eat, or go for a walk.
steps.tip: After 4 cycles, you deserve a longer break!

tips.name: No Phone Rule
tips.description: Keep phone away during work sessions

steps.title: Put phone away
steps.description: Keep your phone in another room.
steps.fullDescription: Physically place your phone in another room. Out of sight, out of mind. This removes the temptation to check notifications.
steps.tip: Use an actual alarm clock instead of your phone.

steps.title: Use Focus Mode
steps.description: Enable Do Not Disturb on all devices.
steps.fullDescription: Enable Do Not Disturb mode on all your devices. Block distracting apps and websites.
steps.tip: Most phones have a 'Focus Mode' feature.

steps.title: Check on breaks
steps.description: Only check your phone during breaks.
steps.fullDescription: Only check your phone during scheduled breaks. This trains your brain to focus without constant interruptions.
steps.tip: Set a timer for your phone check.

tips.name: One Task at a Time
tips.description: Focus on one thing. Multitasking reduces productivity.

steps.title: Write down your task
steps.description: Be specific about what you need to do.
steps.fullDescription: Write down exactly what you need to accomplish. Being specific helps your brain focus on one thing without confusion.
steps.tip: Use a notebook or digital task manager.

steps.title: Block distractions
steps.description: Close all unnecessary tabs and apps.
steps.fullDescription: Close all unnecessary browser tabs and apps. Keep only what you need for your current task. Use website blockers if needed.
steps.tip: Use website blockers like Freedom or Cold Turkey.

steps.title: Work until timer rings
steps.description: Do not switch tasks.
steps.fullDescription: Do not switch tasks until the timer rings. If a new task comes to mind, write it down and return to your current task.
steps.tip: The 2-minute rule: if it takes less than 2 minutes, do it now.

tips.name: Take Breaks Seriously
tips.description: Breaks help you recharge and maintain focus.

steps.title: Stand up and stretch
steps.description: Move your body to improve blood flow.
steps.fullDescription: Stand up from your desk. Stretch your arms, legs, and back. This improves blood flow and reduces muscle tension.
steps.tip: Set a timer to remind you to stand up every hour.

steps.title: Drink water
steps.description: Stay hydrated to maintain concentration.
steps.fullDescription: Drink a glass of water. Dehydration can cause fatigue and reduce concentration. Keep a water bottle at your desk.
steps.tip: Aim for 8 glasses of water per day.

steps.title: Deep breaths
steps.description: Reset your mind with breathing exercises.
steps.fullDescription: Take 5 deep breaths. Inhale slowly through your nose, hold for a moment, exhale through your mouth. This reduces stress and clears your mind.
steps.tip: Try the 4-7-8 breathing technique.

steps.title: Block 90 minutes
steps.description: Schedule 90 minutes of uninterrupted time.
steps.fullDescription: Schedule 90 minutes in your calendar when you won't be disturbed. Turn off all notifications and close your door.
steps.tip: Inform colleagues that you won't be available.

steps.title: Eliminate all distractions
steps.description: Remove everything that can distract you.
steps.fullDescription: Put your phone away. Close all browser tabs not needed for work. Use website blockers.
steps.tip: Use apps like Freedom or Cold Turkey.

steps.title: Focus deeply on one task
steps.description: Work on a single cognitively demanding task.
steps.fullDescription: Work on a single task that requires deep concentration. This could be writing, coding, studying, or creative work.
steps.tip: Don't check email or social media during this time.

steps.title: Wake up early
steps.description: Wake up at the same time every day.
steps.fullDescription: Wake up at the same time every day. Avoid hitting snooze. Open curtains to let natural light in.
steps.tip: Place your alarm across the room.

steps.title: Drink water and stretch
steps.description: Hydrate and wake up your body.
steps.fullDescription: Drink a glass of water first thing. Stretch your body for 5 minutes to wake up your muscles.
steps.tip: Keep a water bottle by your bed.

steps.title: Plan your day
steps.description: Write down your top 3 priorities.
steps.fullDescription: Write down your top 3 priorities for the day. Review your calendar. Set intentions for what you want to accomplish.
steps.tip: Use a notebook or digital planner.

steps.title: Find a quiet place
steps.description: Sit comfortably in a quiet space.
steps.fullDescription: Find a quiet place where you won't be disturbed. Sit comfortably with your back straight.
steps.tip: Use a cushion or chair.

steps.title: Focus on your breath
steps.description: Pay attention to your breathing.
steps.fullDescription: Close your eyes. Focus on your breath as it goes in and out. When your mind wanders, gently bring it back to your breath.
steps.tip: Start with 5-10 minutes daily.

steps.title: Gradually return
steps.description: Slowly open your eyes.
steps.fullDescription: After the timer rings, slowly open your eyes. Take a moment to notice how you feel before standing up.
steps.tip: Use a meditation app like Headspace or Calm.

steps.title: Set a consistent bedtime
steps.description: Go to bed at the same time every night.
steps.fullDescription: Set a bedtime and stick to it, even on weekends. Aim for 7-9 hours of sleep.
steps.tip: Use a sleep tracking app.

steps.title: No screens before bed
steps.description: Avoid phones and computers 1 hour before bed.
steps.fullDescription: Avoid phones, tablets, and computers 1 hour before bed. The blue light disrupts melatonin production.
steps.tip: Read a book instead.

steps.title: Create a relaxing routine
steps.description: Wind down with calming activities.
steps.fullDescription: Create a relaxing bedtime routine: dim lights, listen to calm music, read, or take a warm bath.
steps.tip: Keep your bedroom cool and dark.

tips.name: Goal Setting
tips.description: Set SMART goals for better results.

steps.title: Make it Specific
steps.description: Clearly define what you want to achieve.
steps.fullDescription: Don't just say 'I want to exercise'. Say 'I will walk 30 minutes every morning'.
steps.tip: Use the 5 W's: Who, What, Where, When, Why.

steps.title: Make it Measurable
steps.description: Track your progress with numbers.
steps.fullDescription: Make sure you can measure your progress. Track your progress daily or weekly.
steps.tip: Use a habit tracker app.

steps.title: Set a deadline
steps.description: Give yourself a time limit.
steps.fullDescription: Set a realistic deadline for achieving your goal. Break down large goals into smaller milestones.
steps.tip: Review your goals weekly.

tips.name: Eat the Frog
tips.description: Do the hardest task first thing in the morning.

steps.title: Identify your frog
steps.description: Find the hardest task on your list.
steps.fullDescription: At the end of your day, write down the most important and challenging task for tomorrow. This is your 'frog'.
steps.tip: Be honest about what's hard.

steps.title: Eat it first
steps.description: Do the hardest task before anything else.
steps.fullDescription: When you start your day, do this task first. Don't check email or social media until it's done.
steps.tip: Block your calendar for this task.

tips.name: Learn to Say No
tips.description: Protect your time by declining non-essential requests.

steps.title: Evaluate the request
steps.description: Ask yourself if it aligns with your goals.
steps.fullDescription: Before saying yes, ask: Does this align with my priorities? Do I have time? Will I regret saying yes?
steps.tip: Don't answer immediately. Say 'Let me check my calendar'.

steps.title: Say no politely
steps.description: Decline without feeling guilty.
steps.fullDescription: Say 'Thank you for thinking of me, but I can't take this on right now'. Offer an alternative if possible.
steps.tip: Practice saying no in the mirror.

tips.name: Accountability Partner
tips.description: Share goals with someone who checks on you.

steps.title: Find a partner
steps.description: Choose someone reliable.
steps.fullDescription: Find a trusted friend, colleague, or family member who will hold you accountable. Share your goals with them.
steps.tip: Choose someone who will be honest with you.

steps.title: Weekly check-in
steps.description: Schedule regular progress updates.
steps.fullDescription: Schedule a weekly 10-minute call or message exchange. Share your progress, challenges, and next week's goals.
steps.tip: Use a shared document to track progress.

steps.title: Celebrate wins together
steps.description: Celebrate when you achieve goals.
steps.fullDescription: When you achieve a milestone, celebrate together. This could be a coffee, a meal, or just a congratulatory message.
steps.tip: Celebrate progress, not just perfection.

tips.name: Brain Dump
tips.description: Write everything on your mind to clear mental space.

steps.title: Set a timer for 10 minutes
steps.description: Give yourself time to write freely.
steps.fullDescription: Set a timer for 10 minutes. Don't think about formatting or grammar.
steps.tip: Use a notebook or a blank document.

steps.title: Write everything down
steps.description: Don't filter or organize.
steps.fullDescription: Write down everything that's on your mind: tasks, worries, ideas, reminders. Don't judge or filter.
steps.tip: If you get stuck, write 'I don't know what to write' until ideas come.

steps.title: Review and organize
steps.description: Sort your thoughts into categories.
steps.fullDescription: After the timer, review what you wrote. Sort tasks: do now, delegate, schedule, delete. Identify patterns or recurring worries.
steps.tip: Use different colored pens for different categories.

tips.name: 2-Minute Rule
tips.description: If a task takes less than 2 minutes, do it immediately.

steps.title: Identify quick tasks
steps.description: Look for tasks that take 2 minutes or less.
steps.fullDescription: Throughout your day, notice tasks that take 2 minutes or less: replying to an email, washing a dish, filing a document.
steps.tip: Keep a running list of quick tasks.

steps.title: Do them now
steps.description: Don't postpone small tasks.
steps.fullDescription: When you identify a quick task, do it immediately. Don't add it to your to-do list. Don't postpone.
steps.tip: This prevents small tasks from piling up.
